_description: Reference: dynamic chart block (W5.3) — server-side aggregation + Chart.js v4 client-side rendering. Aggregates any collection (posts / authors / IDAP businesses) by a categorical or temporal field, then ships the Chart.js config baked into the rendered HTML so the client just hydrates Chart.js against `<canvas data-chart-config>`. Sparkline-in-small-container handled via per-type CSS sizing (min-height 32px, height 48px, width 100%).
_DEPENDENCY_CALLOUT_FOR_AGENTS: Chart.js v4.4.6 — NOT Recharts. Plan §9.4 originally proposed Recharts but the renderer is SSR Liquid HTML with zero React; Recharts is React-native and would require bundling React + DOM into every tenant Worker. Chart.js loads via the existing CDN allowlist (chart.js@4.4.6) + AUX-1 hydration runner polls `window.Chart` before invoking the seed JS shim. Do NOT replace Chart.js with Recharts / D3 / ECharts / Highcharts in any tenant page or component spec — the renderer pipeline does not support React or large bespoke libs. The chartjs CDN allowlist row is the single point of truth (migration 113 + 228).

data_binding._max_items_ceiling: 500 — SQL LIMIT 500 hard cap as defence-in-depth vs the Pydantic le=500 validator on the request schema. Larger requests 422 at the validation layer.

_chart_types_available.line: Time-series + continuous numeric. Best for posts.published_at monthly counts, traffic over time.
_chart_types_available.bar: Categorical comparison. Best for top-N by category (top authors, country counts, rating buckets).
_chart_types_available.pie: Share-of-whole. Best for ≤8 segments; prefer bar above 8.
_chart_types_available.area: Stacked time-series. Same as line but filled — good for compositional change over time.
_chart_types_available.sparkline: Inline tiny chart, no axes / legend / tooltips. Best for KPI cards. Per-type CSS sizing (min-height 32px / height 48px / width 100%) so it fits any container.

_aggregations_available.count: COUNT(*) — works on any source. No `value_field` needed.
_aggregations_available.sum: SUM(value_field) — requires `value_field` to be a numeric column.
_aggregations_available.avg: AVG(value_field) — requires `value_field` to be a numeric column.

_group_by_examples: month(published_at) — buckets by calendar month. Auto-formats axis labels.
author_name — categorical group by string column.
rating::int — bucket numeric to integer for histogram-style bars.
props.title: Posts published per month

_supported_sources: posts (content_pages.posts) — fields: published_at, author_id, status, view_count
idap.businesses (norm_cli_<id>.businesses) — fields: city_slug, country_code, rating, reviews_count, lat, lng
Any registered `content_data_sources` row with a `metric_fields` JSONB allowlist
_anti_patterns: Do NOT request a chart with > 500 grouped buckets — the SQL cap rejects it. Use a coarser `group_by` (year() instead of month()) or add a `filter` to narrow first.
Sparkline charts must NOT have `title` / `y_label` / `x_label` props set — they're stripped at render time anyway, but cleaner if the author payload omits them.

Chart.js dependency is auto-loaded by AUX-1 hydration runner from the CDN allowlist. Do NOT manually `<script src=...>` Chart.js in your tenant — the runner deduplicates and resolves load-order automatically.
